summary: An autonomous executor runs long sessions without stalling by writing matchable commands and an allowlist for the routine, while deliberately keeping the gates — identity, destruction, secrets, boundary-class design — that it must not cross.

rules.rule: The allowlist must never cover identity change (forge login/switch), destruction (recursive delete, force-push, hard reset), secret reads, or arbitrary-execution wildcards — these stay prompted or denied on purpose, even under bypass.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: For boundary-class work (identity, access control, security, sensitive-data handling) the executor presents its plan to the coordinator and proceeds only after approval — the plan is approved before the sensitive code is written, never only the draft PR after it.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: The executor proposes, it does not dispose: it opens a draft PR, never merges, never self-approves, and does not edit the architect's governance instruments (the present-mirror, the decision register).

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Before launching work as a single sequential thread, evaluate the wave at the dispatch point: if ≥2 units are READY and INDEPENDENT, fan out by default (one executor per piece, each in its own worktree); if not, name the one blocker — sequencing is the declared exception, never the lazy default.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Before scaffolding any piece, fetch and re-check the current integration branch and in-flight branches/PRs in that area — branching from a stale head risks duplicating work another executor already landed.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: A use-by-default tooling expectation must not be hardened into a required CI/merge gate unless deliberately decided as one; when the environment cannot provide a provisioned tool, proceed without it and document why — never a silent skip.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate
